name: grace_full_lifecycle_sensor_still_open
description: Warm day. Sensor opens at 13:00 (pause). Sensor closes at 13:10 (grace starts, 5-min grace). At 13:12 sensor opens again while grace is active — grace blocks re-pause. Grace expires at 13:15 with sensor still open → re-pause fires.
unsupported_reason: Integration-tier: the grace-blocking-re-pause behavior (sensor_open during active grace returns no_action rather than re-pausing) depends on the coordinator's _grace_active flag, which is set and cleared by async_call_later timers in _handle_contact_sensor_change. The Tier-A harness runs the production AutomationEngine headlessly without the coordinator timer/listener layer; grace_active is never set by the harness, so sensor_open during what the scenario intends to be the grace window does not see the block. The re-pause-on-grace-expiry path (grace_end with sensor open) needs the coordinator grace timer to fire _check_grace_expiry. Deferred to Tier-B HeadlessTarry harness (#236 follow-on) which wires the full coordinator state-listener/timer layer.
notes: Tests the grace period full lifecycle: pause → grace → sensor opens during grace (blocked) → re-pause on grace expiry.
Gap 2 (grace expiry re-check) IS already implemented in simulate.py — grace_end event checks sensors_open and re-pauses if any are open.
All 4 assertions are logic-track: (1) initial sensor_open → paused; (2) sensor_close → resumed; (3) sensor_open during grace → no_action (grace active); (4) grace_end with sensor open → re-paused.
manual_grace_seconds=300 is a config hint for documentation; actual grace timer logic lives in coordinator.py.

events.note: Hot afternoon. Indoor above comfort_cool=74F. Outdoor too warm for nat vent.
Back door opens. Outdoor 88F > indoor 76F — nat vent not eligible. Automation pauses.
Door closes. All sensors closed — automation resumes and grace period begins (5 min = 300s).
Grace period marker: grace_active=True for 5 minutes (ends at 13:15).
Door opens again at 13:12, while grace is still active (expires at 13:15). Grace should block re-pause.
Grace period expires at 13:15. Sensor is still open — should re-pause automation.

assertions.reason: Sensor opens with outdoor (88F) > indoor (76F) — nat vent ineligible. Automation pauses.
All sensors closed — automation resumes. Grace period begins.
Sensor opens during active grace period — grace blocks re-pause. Coordinator _handle_contact_sensor_change() checks _grace_active and suppresses re-pause.
Grace expires with sensor still open → re-pause fires. Coordinator _check_grace_expiry() checks sensors_open and re-pauses if any sensor is open.

verdict.summary: Grace period blocks re-pause during active window; grace expiry with open sensor triggers re-pause
verdict.observed_behavior: paused (13:00) → resumed (13:10) → grace blocks re-pause (13:12) → re-paused on grace expiry (13:15)
verdict.expected_behavior: Grace period prevents rapid pause/resume oscillation; re-pause fires if sensor is still open when grace expires
